metadata.parallel-paths: P4 can run after P1; P6 after P3

phase.name: Audit and Fix Prerequisites
phase.description: Verify all prerequisites: mlruns intact, checkpoints loadable, data splits valid, viz module importable, and fix any blockers before running real data.

task.name: Verify MLflow runs are intact and checkpoints loadable
task.description: Write a diagnostic script (scripts/diagnose_mlruns.py) that: 1. Lists all experiments with run counts 2. For each run in dynunet_loss_variation_v2: verify checkpoint files exist, load state_dict headers (torch.load with weights_only=True), print param counts 3. Verify metric files are non-empty (eval_fold*_dsc, eval_fold*_centreline_dsc) 4. Check for champion tags (expect: NONE currently exist) 5. Print summary: "N runs, M checkpoints, K metrics, champion_tags: {yes/no}"
task.test: Run script, all checkpoints load, all metrics present

task.name: Verify data splits match real volumes
task.description: Load configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json, verify every volume_id listed maps to a real .nii.gz file in data/raw/minivess/imagesTr/ and labelsTr/. Print: - Total volumes discovered: N (expect 70) - Volumes in splits: M (expect 70) - Missing from disk: list (expect empty) - Orphaned on disk: list (expect empty) Add this check to scripts/diagnose_mlruns.py.
task.test: All 70 volumes present, zero missing, zero orphaned

task.name: Fix viz module to accept real ComparisonTable data
task.description:
The viz module (generate_all_figures.py) ONLY uses _demo_comparison_table(). Modify generate_figure() and generate_all_figures() to accept an optional `comparison_table: ComparisonTable | None` parameter. When provided, use it instead of demo data. When None, fall back to demo data for testing.
Also modify each _gen_* function to accept optional ComparisonTable. This is the bridge between real MLflow data and figure generation.
task.test: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_generate_all_figures.py -x -q (add test: generate_figure("loss_comparison", comparison_table=real_table) produces .png)

task.name: Wire format_comparison_latex into analysis flow
task.description: The format_comparison_latex() function exists in comparison.py but is never called by analysis_flow.py. Add a step in run_analysis_flow() after the comparison generation step that: 1. Calls format_comparison_latex(table) to get LaTeX string 2. Saves it to {output_dir}/comparison_table.tex 3. Also calls format_comparison_markdown(table) and saves to {output_dir}/comparison_table.md 4. Returns both paths in the flow result dict
task.test: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_analysis_flow.py -x -q (add test: mock analysis flow produces .tex and .md files)

phase.name: Champion Tagging on Real Experiments
phase.description:
Run champion_tagger.py on the real dynunet_loss_variation_v2 experiment to tag the 3 champion categories (balanced, topology, overlap). This is a PREREQUISITE for deploy flow and analysis flow to work with real data.
Currently: ZERO champion tags exist in mlruns/.

task.name: Run rank-then-aggregate champion selection on dynunet_loss_variation_v2
task.description: Create scripts/tag_champions.py that: 1. Loads all 4 runs from dynunet_loss_variation_v2 (experiment_id=843896622863223169) 2. Extracts eval metrics (eval_fold*_dsc, eval_fold*_centreline_dsc, eval_fold*_measured_masd) 3. Runs rank_then_aggregate() to select champions: - balanced: rank-aggregate across DSC + clDice - topology: best clDice (expected: cbdice_cldice at 0.906) - overlap: best DSC (expected: dice_ce at 0.824) 4. Calls write_champion_tags_filesystem() to persist tags 5. Prints champion assignments with metrics
task.test: Run script, then verify: - find mlruns/843896622863223169 -name "champion_*" → 3+ tag files - Each tag file contains a valid run_id

task.name: Run champion tagging on dynunet_half_width_v1
task.description: Extend scripts/tag_champions.py to also tag the half-width experiment (experiment_id=859817033030295110). This validates cross-architecture champion selection. Use same 3 categories.
task.test: Champion tags written for both experiments

task.name: Verify champion discovery finds tagged runs
task.description: After tagging, verify that deploy_champion_discovery.discover_champions() correctly finds all 3 categories. Write a short test or script call: ```python from minivess.pipeline.deploy_champion_discovery import discover_champions champions = discover_champions(mlruns_dir, experiment_id="843896622863223169") assert len(champions) == 3 # balanced, topology, overlap ```
task.test: discover_champions returns 3 ChampionModel objects

phase.name: Run Data Flow (Flow 1) on Real MiniVess Dataset
phase.description: Execute Flow 1 with real data. Verify pair discovery, validation, quality gate, and K-fold splits match what training used. Persist artifacts to outputs/.

task.name: Run data flow end-to-end on MiniVess 70 volumes
task.description: Create scripts/run_data_flow_real.py that: 1. Calls run_data_flow(data_dir=Path("data/raw/minivess"), n_folds=3, seed=42) 2. Asserts: result.quality_passed is True 3. Asserts: len(result.pairs) == 70 4. Asserts: len(result.splits) == 3 5. Saves splits to outputs/data_flow/splits.json 6. Saves provenance to outputs/data_flow/provenance.json 7. Saves validation report to outputs/data_flow/validation_report.json 8. Compares generated splits with configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json — should match 9. Logs provenance to MLflow (data_n_volumes, data_hash, etc.)
task.test: Script runs successfully, outputs/ contains 3 JSON files, splits match configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json exactly

task.name: Profile dataset and save DatasetProfile
task.description: Extend scripts/run_data_flow_real.py to also run scan_dataset() from profiler.py: 1. Compute VolumeStats for all 70 volumes 2. Build DatasetProfile with shapes, spacings, outliers 3. Save to outputs/data_flow/dataset_profile.json 4. Print summary: min_shape, max_shape, outlier volumes, total size 5. Verify mv02 is flagged as outlier (4.97 μm spacing)
task.test: Dataset profile saved, mv02 flagged as outlier

phase.name: Run Analysis Flow (Flow 3) on Real Trained Models
phase.description:
This is the CORE phase. Run the analysis flow on dynunet_loss_variation_v2 (4 losses × 3 folds × 100 epochs) with real checkpoint loading, real inference on validation volumes, real metric computation, and real artifact generation.
OUTPUTS: per-volume metrics JSON, comparison tables (MD + LaTeX), paper figures, champion rankings, ensemble evaluation, Parquet export.
RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS: GPU inference on 70 volumes × 4 models × 3 folds. Estimated: 30-60 minutes with RTX 2070 Super.

task.name: Create master analysis runner script
task.description:
Create scripts/run_analysis_flow_real.py that: 1. Sets output_dir = Path("outputs/analysis") 2. Configures AnalysisFlowConfig with: - experiment_name = "dynunet_loss_variation_v2" - mlruns_dir = Path("mlruns") - data_dir = Path("data/raw/minivess") - splits_path = Path("configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json") - output_dir = outputs/analysis/dynunet_loss_variation_v2/ - metrics = ["dsc", "centreline_dsc", "measured_masd", "hd95", "nsd", "cldice"] - bootstrap_n = 1000 (for CIs) 3. Calls run_analysis_flow() — this triggers ALL 9 tasks: load artifacts → build ensembles → evaluate → compare → tag → report 4. Saves ALL outputs to disk (not just returns them) 5. Error handling: catch and report per-task failures without aborting entire flow
Key difference from mock tests: this loads REAL checkpoints (~65MB each), runs REAL GPU inference on REAL NIfTI volumes, computes REAL metrics.
task.test: Script starts, loads checkpoints, begins inference

task.name: Per-volume metric persistence to MLflow and JSON
task.description:
After evaluation, for each model × fold: 1. Call FoldResult.save_per_volume_json() to save per-volume metrics to outputs/analysis/per_volume_metrics/{loss}_{fold}.json 2. Call ExperimentTracker.log_per_volume_metrics() to persist as MLflow artifact 3. Verify roundtrip: load JSON, check volume IDs match, check metric values
Expected output: 4 losses × 3 folds = 12 JSON files, each containing ~23-24 volumes with dsc, centreline_dsc, measured_masd per volume.
task.test: 12 per-volume JSON files in outputs/analysis/per_volume_metrics/

task.name: Generate comparison table (Markdown + LaTeX)
task.description:
After all models evaluated: 1. Call build_comparison_table(eval_results) with REAL evaluation data 2. Call format_comparison_markdown(table) → save to outputs/analysis/comparison_table.md 3. Call format_comparison_latex(table) → save to outputs/analysis/comparison_table.tex 4. Call compute_all_pairwise_comparisons() → paired bootstrap significance tests 5. Call format_significance_matrix_markdown() → save to outputs/analysis/significance_matrix.md 6. Verify: LaTeX has \toprule, bold best values, all 4 loss names present 7. Verify: Markdown is human-readable in terminal
This produces the ACTUAL paper table — not mock data.
task.test: comparison_table.tex, comparison_table.md, significance_matrix.md all exist and contain real metric values (DSC ~0.78-0.82, clDice ~0.83-0.91)

task.name: Generate paper-quality figures from real data
task.description:
After comparison table is built: 1. Call generate_all_figures(output_dir, comparison_table=real_table) where real_table is built from actual eval results 2. Generate 4 core figures: - loss_comparison.png: bar chart of DSC/clDice per loss (real values) - fold_heatmap.png: per-fold metric matrix (real values) - metric_correlation.png: DSC vs clDice scatter (real values) - sensitivity_heatmap.png: factorial analysis (real values) 3. Also generate training curve plots from metric_history.json: - training_curves.png: train/val loss over 100 epochs per loss 4. Export in multiple formats: PNG (300 DPI) + SVG (vector for paper) 5. Save alongside JSON reproducibility data
Each figure must contain REAL experiment numbers, not demo data.
task.test: 4+ PNG files in outputs/analysis/figures/, non-zero size, visual inspection shows real metric ranges

task.name: DuckDB extraction and Parquet export
task.description:
After analysis is complete: 1. Call extract_runs_to_duckdb(mlruns_dir, experiment_id, db_path="outputs/analysis/mlruns.duckdb") 2. Export 3 Parquet files: - outputs/analysis/parquet/eval_metrics.parquet (fold_id, loss_name, metric_name, point_estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper) - outputs/analysis/parquet/training_metrics.parquet (run_id, loss_name, epoch, metric_name, value) - outputs/analysis/parquet/champion_summary.parquet (category, run_id, loss_name, primary_metric, value) 3. Verify DuckDB queries return expected data: - query_cross_loss_means() returns 4 rows (one per loss) - query_best_per_metric() returns results for each metric - query_champion_runs() returns 3 rows (one per category)
This creates the data lake for downstream BI/paper analytics.
task.test: 3 .parquet files in outputs/analysis/parquet/, loadable with pandas, correct row counts, DuckDB file is ~1-5 MB

task.name: Generate final analysis report (Markdown)
task.description: Combine all artifacts into a comprehensive analysis report: 1. The flow's generate_report() task produces markdown 2. Save to outputs/analysis/analysis_report.md 3. Should contain: experiment summary, per-loss results, comparison table, champion selections, ensemble performance, promotion recommendations 4. Copy to docs/results/dynunet_loss_variation_v2_analysis_flow_report.md (alongside the existing hand-written report)
task.test: analysis_report.md exists, contains all 4 loss names, contains champion info, >100 lines

phase.name: Analysis on Half-Width and Debug Experiments
phase.description: Run the same analysis pipeline on dynunet_half_width_v1 and dynunet_all_losses_debug to generate multi-experiment comparison.

task.name: Run analysis on dynunet_half_width_v1
task.description: Same as P3-T1 through P3-T6 but for experiment_id=859817033030295110. Output to: outputs/analysis/dynunet_half_width_v1/ This gives us width ablation comparison data.
task.test: All artifacts generated for half-width experiment

task.name: Generate cross-experiment comparison table
task.description: Create a cross-experiment comparison that includes: 1. Full-width DynUNet (4 losses, 100 epochs) 2. Half-width DynUNet (4 losses, 100 epochs) Combine into single ComparisonTable with loss names prefixed by width. Save: outputs/analysis/cross_experiment_comparison.tex Save: outputs/analysis/cross_experiment_comparison.md This shows the impact of model capacity on each loss function.
task.test: Cross-experiment table contains 8 rows (4 losses × 2 widths)

phase.name: Run Deploy Flow (Flow 4) with Real Champions
phase.description: Export champions to ONNX, import to BentoML, generate deployment artifacts. This produces the FIRST EVER ONNX models from real training.

task.name: Run deploy flow end-to-end with real champions
task.description:
Create scripts/run_deploy_flow_real.py that: 1. Configures DeployConfig: - mlruns_dir = Path("mlruns") - output_dir = Path("outputs/deploy") - onnx_opset = 17 - experiment_id = "843896622863223169" 2. Calls deploy_flow(config, experiment_id) 3. Expects 3 champions (balanced, topology, overlap) from Phase 1 tags 4. Exports 3 ONNX models to outputs/deploy/onnx/ 5. Generates bentofile.yaml + docker-compose.yaml + DEPLOY_README.md 6. Verifies ONNX models load with onnx.load() (checker) 7. If onnxruntime available: runs test inference to verify output shapes
NOTE: BentoML import may fail if bentoml[onnx] not installed. Script should handle this gracefully and report which steps succeeded.
task.test: 3 ONNX files in outputs/deploy/onnx/, bentofile.yaml generated, docker-compose.yaml contains 3 services

task.name: Verify ONNX vs PyTorch parity
task.description:
For each champion ONNX model: 1. Load PyTorch checkpoint 2. Load ONNX model 3. Run inference on 1 validation volume (same input) 4. Compare outputs: np.allclose(pytorch_output, onnx_output, atol=1e-3) 5. Report max absolute difference per champion 6. Call verify_onnx_vs_pytorch() from deploy_verification.py
This validates the ONNX export didn't introduce numerical drift.
task.test: All 3 champions pass ONNX parity check (max diff < 1e-3)

phase.name: Run Dashboard Flow (Flow 5) with Real Data
phase.description: Generate the Everything Dashboard with actual data from all upstream flows.

task.name: Run dashboard flow with real upstream results
task.description:
Create scripts/run_dashboard_flow_real.py that: 1. Collects results from Phases 2-5: - Data section: 70 volumes, quality passed, 3 external registered - Config section: dynaconf env, experiment config name, model profile - Model section: DynUNet architecture, param count from checkpoint, loss=cbdice_cldice, onnx_exported=True, champion=balanced - Pipeline section: flow results from each phase, trigger="manual" 2. Calls run_dashboard_flow(sections, output_dir="outputs/dashboard") 3. Verifies: everything_dashboard_report.md has all 4 sections 4. Verifies: everything_dashboard_metadata.json is valid JSON
This produces a comprehensive one-page summary of the entire pipeline.
task.test: Report and metadata files exist in outputs/dashboard/, report > 50 lines, JSON valid, all sections populated

phase.name: End-to-End Pipeline Trigger Chain
phase.description: Wire all 5 flows through PipelineTriggerChain and run the full sequence. This is the ultimate verification: data → (training skipped, use existing) → analyze → deploy → dashboard. All with real data and real artifacts.

task.name: Wire trigger chain with all real flows
task.description:
Create scripts/run_full_pipeline.py that: 1. Creates PipelineTriggerChain() 2. Registers all flows: - "data": run_data_flow (real MiniVess) - "train": SKIP (use existing dynunet_loss_variation_v2 runs) - "analyze": run_analysis_flow (real evaluation) - "deploy": deploy_flow (real ONNX export) - "dashboard": run_dashboard_flow (real aggregation) 3. Calls chain.run_chain(trigger_source="test/quasi-e2e-debugging", skip_flows=["train"]) 4. Reports per-flow status, duration, errors 5. Saves chain result to outputs/pipeline/trigger_chain_results.json
This validates the full pipeline orchestration contract.
task.test: All 4 flows complete successfully (train skipped), trigger chain results show status=COMPLETED for each

task.name: Verify all artifacts exist and are valid
task.description:
Final verification script (scripts/verify_all_artifacts.py) that: 1. Checks ALL expected artifacts from ALL phases exist on disk 2. Validates file sizes are non-trivial (>100 bytes for JSON/MD/TEX) 3. Validates PNG files are loadable (Pillow or matplotlib) 4. Validates Parquet files are loadable (pandas.read_parquet) 5. Validates ONNX files are loadable (onnx.load + onnx.checker) 6. Validates DuckDB file has expected tables 7. Prints comprehensive artifact inventory:
ARTIFACT INVENTORY ══════════════════ Data Flow: 3 JSON files (splits, provenance, profile) Analysis Flow: 12 per-volume JSONs, 3 tables (MD+TEX+SIG), 5 figures, 3 Parquet, 1 DuckDB, 1 report Deploy Flow: 3 ONNX models, 3 deployment configs Dashboard Flow: 1 report, 1 metadata JSON Pipeline: 1 trigger chain result ────────────── TOTAL: 35+ artifacts
task.test: All artifacts present and valid, zero missing, zero corrupt

task.name: Create paper artifact summary for academic submission
task.description:
Create outputs/paper_artifacts/README.md that: 1. Lists all figures with captions for paper: - Figure 1: Loss comparison (DSC, clDice, MASD across 4 losses) - Figure 2: Per-fold heatmap (3 folds × 4 losses × 3 metrics) - Figure 3: Metric correlation (DSC vs clDice scatter) - Figure 4: Training curves (100 epochs, 4 losses) 2. Lists all tables: - Table 1: Main comparison table (comparison_table.tex) - Table 2: Statistical significance (significance_matrix.md) - Table 3: Cross-experiment comparison (full vs half width) 3. Lists all supplementary data: - Per-volume metrics (12 JSON files) - DuckDB analytics database - Parquet exports 4. Includes LaTeX \input{} commands for tables 5. Copies all paper-ready artifacts to outputs/paper_artifacts/
This is the "here's everything you need to write the paper" directory.
task.test: outputs/paper_artifacts/ contains all figures (PNG+SVG), all tables (TEX), README.md with LaTeX inclusion commands

per-phase.command: uv run ruff check src/ tests/ scripts/

risk.description: GPU OOM during inference on all 70 volumes
risk.mitigation: Use sliding window inference with small roi_size=(32,32,32). Process one volume at a time. torch.no_grad() context. Clear CUDA cache between volumes.

risk.description: onnxruntime not installed → ONNX validation fails
risk.mitigation: Check import onnxruntime at script start. If missing, skip runtime validation but still verify onnx.load() structural check passes. Report clearly which validations were skipped.

risk.description: Analysis flow takes too long (>2 hours for all models)
risk.mitigation: Support --quick flag that evaluates only fold 0 instead of all 3. Support --loss flag to run single loss function only. Progress bar with tqdm for volume-level progress.

risk.description: Existing mlruns metric files have different schema than expected
risk.mitigation: Diagnose script (P0-T1) catches schema mismatches early. All metric access goes through MLflow API, not raw file parsing.

risk.description: State dict key prefix mismatch when loading checkpoints
risk.mitigation: Use _strip_state_dict_prefix() from deploy_onnx_export.py. Try multiple prefix patterns: "module.", "model.", "conv."

crash-recovery.principle: Each script is idempotent — re-running overwrites previous outputs. Scripts check for existing outputs and skip completed steps when --resume flag is passed. Phase dependencies are tracked via marker files in outputs/.done/
